title: Free Province of Elmswick
content:
The Dorsaly Concord (1998) was a diplomatic agreement in which Kira Noxley, chief negotiator of Free Province of Elmswick, helped finalize terms at Elmswick.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Elmswick conference hall include Kira Noxley's opening address and the ratification roll call.

Signing and terms.

The Dorsaly Concord was signed in Elmswick on 14 September 1998. The agreement established trade corridors and a joint commission chaired initially by Kira Noxley.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1999 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Elmswick basin.

Background.

By the early 1998s, diplomats from Free Province of Elmswick sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Elmswick.

Negotiations.

Kira Noxley, serving as chief negotiator, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1997. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Dorsaly Concord as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Kira Noxley's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Free Province of Elmswick praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Elmswick were not sufficiently protected.
